sections.heading: Problem Statement
sections.content: Code changes faster than documentation. Without automated staleness detection, neither developers nor agents can know whether the description of a function is still accurate — they either read stale docs and make wrong assumptions, or re-read the source code every time, defeating the purpose of the index.

sections.heading: User Stories
sections.content:
**US-ST-01 — Know what's out of date**
As a developer, I want to know which functions have code that changed without matching doc updates so that I can catch documentation drift before it merges.

**US-ST-02 — Fast check on unchanged projects**
As a developer, I want staleness detection to run in under one second on unchanged projects so that I can use it as a pre-commit hook without friction.

**US-ST-03 — Trust signal before reading docs**
As an agent, I want to check whether a node's documentation is stale before reading it so that I know whether to trust the existing description or go read the source directly.

**US-ST-04 — Doc section staleness with cause**
As an agent, I want to see which doc sections are CONTENT_UPDATED and which code node triggered it so that I know exactly what prose to update without re-reading every linked function.

**US-ST-05 — Mark clean with attribution**
As an agent, I want to mark a node clean with a reason and my identity so that subsequent agents and the impact report can see what was already verified and why.

**US-ST-06 — Know when tests need review after code changes**
As a developer, I want tests to show LINKED_STALE when the code they validate changed so I know which tests to review after a code change — without the reverse being true: a test change does not affect the validated function's staleness.

**US-ST-07 — LINKED_STALE for parent sections**
As a developer viewing a nested doc section, I want the parent section to show LINKED_STALE when any child section is stale so I can trace staleness down the hierarchy — seeing LINKED_STALE tells me to look inside, where I'll find the actual DESC_UPDATED or CONTENT_UPDATED on the child that needs attention.

sections.heading: Current Capabilities
sections.content:
| Capability | Status |
|---|---|
| Mtime-based fast path in full hash check (skips unchanged files) | Done |
| Per-function AST `code_hash` / `desc_hash` comparison | Done |
| Two-column staleness model — `own_status` (node's own content) and `link_status` (linked nodes) stored as separate columns | Done |
| `CONTENT_UPDATED` own_status (own content changed — code_hash differs) | Done |
| `DESC_UPDATED` own_status (descriptor/heading changed — desc_hash differs) | Done |
| `LINKED_STALE` link_status (dependency changed via `documents` or `validates` edge) | Done |
| `NOT_FOUND` own_status (file or node no longer exists) | Done |
| `VERIFIED` own_status / link_status (baseline matches current hash) | Done |
| `BROKEN_LINK` link_status (linked node not found in index) | Done |
| `UNVERIFIED` removed as visible status (nodes with no history default to VERIFIED) | Done |
| `_now_utc()` helper in `db.py` — single source for ISO timestamp formatting | Done |
| Single staleness writer — `compute_staleness` extracted to shared `staleness.py` module, called by CLI, MCP, and viz | Done |
| `_compute_staleness_from_history()` deleted — history-table path removed entirely | Done |
| `_compute_staleness_for_nodes()` deleted — third MCP implementation removed | Done |
| Persisted `own_status` and `link_status` columns on `nodes` table — write on build/check, read on render/list | Done |
| `get_stale_tests()` DB query uses `subtype='test'` (was `node_type='constraint'`, never matched) | Done |
| `get_stale_doc_sections()` query updated for `subtype='docjson'` (was `node_type='document'`) | Done |
| Composite node staleness inheritance (worst-child via composes edges, shared helper) | Done |
| Composite severity map includes all statuses (`CONTENT_UPDATED`, `LINKED_STALE` no longer silently map to VERIFIED) | Done |
| "Both changed -> CONTENT_UPDATED" fix (was incorrectly falling through to VERIFIED) | Done |
| `cortex check` with `--fail-on` (`none` / `stale` / `any`) | Done |
| `DESC_UPDATED` included in `--fail-on stale` exit condition | Done |
| `LINKED_STALE` in `cortex check` summary and output | Done |
| `cortex mark-clean` with reason and `verified_by` | Done |
| `verified_by` distinguishes `'agent'` from `'human'` | Done |
| Dual-hash verification — `desc_hash_at` column added to `node_verification`, promotion requires both hashes match | Done |
| Doc sections verifiable — `verify_node()` rejection of `node_type='document'` removed (sections are now `atomic_process`) | Done |
| `node_verification.reason` column for free-text verification reason | Done |
| `cortex build --verify-baseline` — one-time baseline acceptance command | Not started |
| LINKED_STALE propagation through composes edges (nested parent sections) | Done |
| `apply_composite_inheritance` node-type awareness (atomic vs composite parents) | Done |

sections.heading: Backlog
sections.content:
**`cortex build --verify-baseline` (US-ST-05)** — One-time command run after a project's initial scan to establish an accepted baseline. Finds every node whose last history row is `INITIAL` (never changed since discovery), writes a `node_verification` row for each with `verified_by="human"` and the current `code_hash_at`. Those nodes promote to `VERIFIED` own_status. Intentionally a one-time operation.

**VCS context enrichment** — Future: include the commit message that triggered a node going stale in the check output. Icebox until VCS integration is designed.
